title: sync load forget to skip the column which is in the tidb_analyze_skip_column_types
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
CREATE TABLE `t1` (
  `id` bigint(20) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
  `content` text,
  PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin;

INSERT INTO `t1` (`content`) VALUES ('这是一段示例文本内容。');
analyze table t1;
set global tidb_analyze_skip_column_types=text;
select * from t1 where content="abc";
```
<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

no sync load to trigger 
### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

sync load to trigger 

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 ( id INTEGER NOT NULL, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, description LONG VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(), PRIMARY KEY (id));
INSERT INTO t2 (name, description) VALUES ('Sample', NULL);
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE name = 'Sample' AND description IS NULL;
key_bug_features: sync load triggers despite skipping specified column types
